step_by_step_thinking:
I agree that option C, "Large proportionate body," does not characterize fetal alcohol syndrome. The key feature here is the contrast between disproportionate physical characteristics seen in other conditions versus the more uniform bodily proportions typically observed in individuals affected by prenatal alcohol exposure. This distinction supports why option C can be excluded from the list of classic symptoms associated with Fetal Alcohol Syndrome. 

Answer Choice: C. Disagree (Option C should exclude)
